# DeadFinder

> Find dead (broken) links in web pages, URL lists, and sitemaps.

This is documentation for DeadFinder, an open-source CLI that finds broken links in web pages, URL lists, and sitemaps. Content is MIT-licensed.

## Pages

- [DeadFinder](https://deadfinder.hahwul.com/): Find dead (broken) links in web pages, URL lists, and sitemaps.
- [About](https://deadfinder.hahwul.com/about/): About DeadFinder

## Getting Started

- [Installation](https://deadfinder.hahwul.com/docs/getting-started/installation/): Install DeadFinder via Homebrew, Docker, prebuilt binary, Nix, or from source.
- [Quick Start](https://deadfinder.hahwul.com/docs/getting-started/quickstart/): Run your first DeadFinder scan and read its output.

## Integration

- [Docker](https://deadfinder.hahwul.com/docs/integration/docker/): ghcr.io/hahwul/deadfinder — multi-arch, cosign-signed, tiny Alpine base.
- [GitHub Action](https://deadfinder.hahwul.com/docs/integration/github-action/): hahwul/deadfinder composite action — inputs, outputs, examples.

## Reference

- [CLI Flags](https://deadfinder.hahwul.com/docs/reference/cli-flags/): Complete reference for every deadfinder option.

## Usage

- [Filtering](https://deadfinder.hahwul.com/docs/usage/filtering/): Regex match/ignore, 3xx inclusion, URL limit.
- [Output Formats](https://deadfinder.hahwul.com/docs/usage/output-formats/): JSON, YAML, TOML, CSV, SARIF, coverage reports, and PNG visualization.
- [Subcommands](https://deadfinder.hahwul.com/docs/usage/subcommands/): url / file / pipe / sitemap / completion / version
